name: Germany
summary: Strong software/AI market, no tuition at public universities, clear study->work->PR route; German needed for fast citizenship.

job-market.summary: Deep demand for software/AI engineers across industry.
job-market.reasoning: Large tech and industrial software sector; EU Blue Card eases hiring of CS graduates.
evidence: EU Blue Card salary thresholds widely met by SW roles

visa-access.summary: Embassy in Dhaka; structured student-visa process with blocked account.
visa-access.reasoning: German Embassy Dhaka processes national visas; requires blocked account and APS certificate for Bangladeshi students.
evidence: German Mission Dhaka issues student visas
APS certificate required for BD applicants

citizenship.summary: Naturalisation possible in ~5 years; dual citizenship allowed since 2024.
citizenship.reasoning: 2024 reform cut standard naturalisation to 5 years and permits dual citizenship; B1 German required.

post-study-work.reasoning: Graduates get an 18-month permit to seek qualified work; may work without restriction during it.

links.title: Jobs after studies (Make it in Germany)

spouse-family.summary: Spouse reunification with broad work rights.
spouse-family.reasoning: Dependent spouses of students/workers can join; spouses of skilled workers generally get unrestricted labour-market access.
evidence: Spouse of skilled worker may work without restriction

msc-study.summary: No tuition at public universities; many English MSc programs.
msc-study.reasoning: Public universities charge no tuition (semester fee only); broad English-taught MSc catalogue; 120 full days/year work.
evidence: 120 full / 240 half work days per year

pr-pathway.summary: Settlement permit in ~21-33 months on Blue Card with German.
pr-pathway.reasoning: EU Blue Card holders reach permanent settlement in 21 months (B1) or 27-33 months (A1), faster than most.
evidence: Settlement permit in 21 months with B1 German on Blue Card

income-cost.summary: Solid net salaries; housing tight in major cities.
income-cost.reasoning: Good engineering salaries; rents high in Munich/Berlin but overall savings possible.

healthcare.summary: Universal statutory health insurance; strong welfare.
healthcare.reasoning: Mandatory statutory health insurance covers residents and students; comprehensive welfare and parental benefits.

culture-language.summary: English works in tech, but German needed for PR/citizenship and daily life.
culture-language.reasoning: Tech workplaces often English; German required for bureaucracy, fast PR (B1) and citizenship.
evidence: German needed for officialdom and naturalisation

safety-law.summary: Strong rule of law and low violent crime.
safety-law.reasoning: High rule-of-law ranking; generally safe; established anti-discrimination protections.
evidence: High WJP Rule of Law index

politics.summary: Stable democracy; some rise in anti-immigration politics.
politics.reasoning: Stable institutions but growing far-right vote share adds modest policy uncertainty.

tax.summary: High income tax and social contributions.
tax.reasoning: Progressive tax plus social contributions reduce take-home pay; some student relief.

muslim-diaspora.summary: Large Muslim and South-Asian communities; halal widely available.
muslim-diaspora.reasoning: Several million Muslims; mosques and halal food common in cities; some anti-Muslim sentiment exists.
evidence: Halal food and mosques in major cities
